what_it_does: Implements a single model-selection resolver, resolve_model, that accepts an intent dict and returns a record with model, base_url, provider, why, satisfied, and kind. It dispatches to three branches: clone (context-size pick for ollama clones), role (effective role binding via a suite), and capability (first provider matching all requested capability tags). It also defines a fail-loud exception, ModelRoutingError.
observations: Heavy docstring frames the module as Phase 1 of issue #71, unifying scattered model-selection seams without changing live behavior yet.
The resolver is registry-is-truth and reuses existing seams: pick_ollama_model_for_context, suite.resolve_role_binding, and suite.capability_providers.
base_url values are sourced from fabric.config; only OLLAMA_DIRECT appears in this file and is noted as informational for clones.
The role branch explicitly preserves and surfaces the satisfied flag from the binding, warning that a default_model floor looks resolved but is not.
Capability matching uses subset inclusion: set(needed) <= set(provider.provides).
All branches return a uniform dict shape; unknown or malformed intents raise ModelRoutingError.
standards_out: The unified resolve_model entry point and its explicit intent/kind contract.
The advisor-caught FLOOR trap: surfacing satisfied=False when a role floors to default_model.
Reuse-don't-parallel: clone branch delegates to the same TIM-RULE picker the clone path already uses.
conventions: Fail-loud via ModelRoutingError instead of silent defaults.
Config-driven endpoints; no hardcoded base URLs except the config-canonical OLLAMA_DIRECT.
Lazy imports inside branch functions to avoid circular issues and keep the module lightweight.
Type hints and from __future__ import annotations.
Uniform result shape across all intent kinds.
concerns: Capability branch returns the first matching provider with no explicit priority or ranking; order depends on suite.capability_providers iteration.
Role branch does not independently verify that the returned model is actually provided by the matched provider; it trusts suite.resolve_role_binding.
Clone branch includes a base_url that the docstring says is not consumed by the actual ollama launch path, included only for shape uniformity.
No behavior-altering callers are wired yet (Phase 1), so the resolver is currently unproven in live firing paths.
notes: This file is the Phase 1 scaffold for a unified model router: a clean intent-in/record-out resolver that exposes whether a role really got a capability match or just hit the default floor.
questions: What exact fields does suite.resolve_role_binding return, and does it always include satisfied, provider, model, base_url, requires, provides, and candidates?
How does suite.capability_providers order its providers, and is that order intended to be the selection priority?
What is the exact signature and return shape of pick_ollama_model_for_context?
Where is OLLAMA_DIRECT defined and what is its value?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose matches actual content: it is a unified resolver that wraps existing seams and does not yet route live traffic, exactly as the Phase 1 docstring describes.
intention_signals: The file is trying to consolidate all model-selection decisions into one inspectable, testable entry point while preserving the existing behavior of three separate seams and making a hidden default-model floor visible.
novelty: The explicit satisfied flag and the accompanying FLOOR warning for role intents, turning a previously silent false-positive resolution into an observable state.
contribution: This file contributes a single intent-driven model resolver and a dedicated exception type, producing uniform, inspectable routing records and exposing whether a role truly matched a provider or merely fell back to a default model, without altering existing routing behavior.
summary_for_embedding: A unified, fail-loud model-selection resolver that maps clone, role, and capability intents to model/base_url/provider records by reusing existing registry seams and surfacing a satisfaction flag.
intention_for_embedding: To consolidate scattered model-selection logic into one verifiable entry point (#71) while preserving current behavior and exposing silent default-model floors.

symbols.does: Exception raised when a model-routing intent cannot be resolved.
symbols.description: A dedicated fail-loud error type used to reject malformed, under-specified, or unresolvable intents rather than returning a silent default model.

symbols.does: Resolves a clone intent by picking an ollama model for the requested context size.
symbols.description: Validates context_tokens, delegates to pick_ollama_model_for_context, and returns a record containing the chosen model plus the canonical OLLAMA_DIRECT base_url for shape uniformity even though the actual clone launch is ollama-native.

symbols.does: Resolves a role intent through the suite's effective role binding.
symbols.description: Calls suite.resolve_role_binding and builds a result that preserves the binding's model, provider, and satisfaction state, explicitly surfacing when a role floors to a default_model because no provider matched.

symbols.does: Resolves a capability intent to the first provider that provides all needed tags.
symbols.description: Normalizes the capability input to a list, filters suite.capability_providers by subset match, and returns the first matching provider's model and endpoint along with a candidates list.

symbols.does: Single entry point that dispatches clone, role, and capability intents to the corresponding branch.
symbols.description: Validates the intent dict and kind, then delegates to _clone_branch, _role_branch, or _capability_branch, requiring a suite for role and capability intents and failing loudly for unknown kinds.
